title: asperula cynanchica var. jordanii
intro: Asperula cynanchica var. jordanii, also known as Jordan's Woodruff, is a variant of the Asperula cynanchica plant species. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean and is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which provides anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, making it useful for relieving pain and reducing inflammation. In herbal preparations, Jordan's Woodruff is often used to treat various health issues, including headaches, fever, and digestive problems, and can be consumed as a tea or used in infusions.
